question: When Jesus is mocked as ‘King of the Jews,’ how do the Gospel writers simultaneously show irony + fulfillment—and which OT royal/servant themes are being braided together?

choices.A: The mockery ironically proclaims true kingship, while the narrative braids Psalm 2’s royal theme with Isaiah 53’s suffering servant theme.
choices.B: The mockery proves Jesus was a failed rebel.
choices.C: It shows the writers opposed the idea of Jesus as king.
choices.D: It has no connection to any OT themes.

teaching.title: Crowned in mockery, enthroned in meaning
teaching.body: The Passion mockery is written with dramatic irony: the insult functions as a truth the mockers don’t understand. The OT background can be read as royal (Psalm 2) and servant-suffering (Isaiah 53) converging—messiahship through suffering, not propaganda power.
